topic.what: Domain-specific contexts exploit LLM safety boundaries through gray zone between benign and harmful inputs, with safety-research contexts triggering general unlock across all harm categories
topic.how: Multi-turn adversarial framework combining safety-research contexts, rapport-building, and contextually reframed queries to extract harmful knowledge
topic.why_matters: Exposes fundamental tension in LLM alignment where knowledge duality creates exploitable gaps; models cannot reject all domain interactions without compromising legitimate professional use cases

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of jailbreak attempts classified as successful by judge scoring 0.8-1.0
characteristics.evidence: Table with 10 models including GPT-5.2, Claude-4.5, Gemini-3
characteristics.context: Across 7 frontier models evaluated on JailbreakBench 100 prompts

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of query variants generated per attack attempt for parallel evaluation
characteristics.context: Two diversification strategies in equal proportion

characteristics.what_itasures: Number of turns required for rapport-building and attack extraction
characteristics.evidence: k=2 rapport-building turns plus attack turn
characteristics.context: Initial turns establish academic framing before pivoting to harmful queries

characteristics.what_it_measures: Domain-aligned context relaxation for domain-relevant threats vs mismatched pairs
characteristics.vs_baseline: Diagonal pattern in heatmaps shows higher scores for aligned pairs
characteristics.evidence: Virology paper preceding virus synthesis query achieves 0.78-0.96 across models
characteristics.context: Probing study on 8 threat categories with 3 models

characteristics.what_it_measures: Single jailbreak research paper effectiveness across all threat domains
characteristics.vs_baseline: Uniform high scores across all 8 domains
characteristics.evidence: Bar charts in Figure 2 show consistent effectiveness
characteristics.context: Demonstrates privileged position of safety-research framing

characteristics.what_it_measures: Activation space location of JARGON queries relative to benign and harmful inputs
characteristics.vs_baseline: Between benign and overtly harmful inputs
characteristics.evidence: Activation space analysis reveals intermediate region

good_for: safety boundary probing for LLM evaluation

novelty: identifies hierarchical vulnerability structure (Vertical vs General Unlocking)
proposes multi-turn framework exploiting authentic safety-research discourse
demonstrates gray zone activation space between benign and harmful
introduces policy-guided safeguard with alignment fine-tuning for defense
achieves >93% ASR across frontier models including GPT-5.2 and Claude-4.5
open_problems: scalable defense strategies beyond proposed safeguard
mechanistic understanding of gray zone refusal decision failures
long-term mitigation of knowledge duality tension
